label: BREAKING
hook: CHINA TELLS FIRMS: IGNORE US SANCTIONS ON IRAN
stat1: China imports ~600k barrels Iranian oil daily
stat2: 52 ships turned back by US naval blockade
stat3: Second largest economy refuses to comply
tts_text: Breaking. China just told its companies to ignore US sanctions on Iran, buying Iranian oil anyway. When the world's second largest economy stops fearing sanctions, the tool stops working.
caption:
China's government just issued official guidance to its companies: keep buying Iranian oil, ignore US sanctions. This isn't a rumor or backroom deal. This is open defiance.

The context matters. The US is actively blockading Iran through the Strait of Hormuz. 52 ships have been turned back. An aircraft carrier is deployed. Trump's team calls it maximum pressure. China's response is maximum indifference.

This is what sanctions erosion looks like in real time. The entire architecture of US secondary sanctions depends on fear: fear of losing access to dollar markets, fear of being blacklisted, fear that compliance pays. When the second largest economy decides that fear doesn't apply to it, the math changes. Trump's maximum pressure just became maximum theater.

, The Clam Letter

#breaking #china #iran #sanctions #hormuz
